samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal. Active low, defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal. Active low, defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal. Triggered to initiate write operations.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal. Triggered to initiate read operations.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input. Configurable width representing the data to be written into the FIFO.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output. Configurable width representing the data read from the FIFO.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="8">Configurable width of the FIFO data bus.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="16">Configurable depth of the FIFO memory.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="waddr_bin" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))-1:0]">Binary representation of the write address pointer.</reg>
        <reg id="raddr_bin" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))-1:0]">Binary representation of the read address pointer.</reg>
        <reg id="wptr" width_description="[3:0]">Gray code representation of the write pointer.</reg>
        <reg id="rptr" width_description="[3:0]">Gray code representation of the read pointer.</reg>
        <reg id="wptr_buff" width_description="[3:0]">Buffer register for synchronized write pointer.</reg>
        <reg id="rptr_buff" width_description="[3:0]">Buffer register for synchronized read pointer.</reg>
        <wire id="wen" width_description="1">Write enable signal for the dual-port RAM.</wire>
        <wire id="ren" width_description="1">Read enable signal for the dual-port RAM.</wire>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Dual-port RAM instantiation
        <instance id="dual_port_RAM" module="dual_port_RAM">
            <port id="wclk" connection="wclk"/>
            <port id="wenc" connection="wen"/>
            <port id="waddr" connection="waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="wdata" connection="wdata[WIDTH-1:0]"/>
            <port id="rclk" connection="rclk"/>
            <port id="renc" connection="ren"/>
            <port id="raddr" connection="raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="rdata" connection="rdata[WIDTH-1:0]"/>
        </instance>
        
        // Write Pointer Logic
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                waddr_bin <= 0;
            end else if (winc) begin
                waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Read Pointer Logic
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                raddr_bin <= 0;
            end else if (rinc) begin
                raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Convert Binary to Gray Code
        always @(*) begin
            wptr = {waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1], ^waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-2:0]};
            rptr = {raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1], ^raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-2:0]};
        end
        
        // Buffer Registers for Synchronization
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_buff <= rptr;
            end
        end
        
        // Full/Empty Signal Logic
        assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_buff[3], rptr_buff[2:0]});
        assign rempty = (wptr_buff == rptr);
        
        // Control Signals for Dual-port RAM
        assign wen = winc && !wfull;
        assign ren = rinc && !rempty;
    </implementation>
</module>
